verdict: PARTIAL — finding-count keeps dropping but score ceiling holds at 6.22 avg. Plateau-call territory: 4 consecutive rounds at this score across baselines (R3, R5, R6, R6+1). Per evolve doc, 3+ rounds without 0.5pt movement → escalate to /pursue Gen-3 (Section/BentoCell primitives, kill legacy 1791-line global.css).

delta_vs_r6: BP-agent critical 1→0, findings 4→4, score 6.0→6.0. Architectural shift complete; per-page CSS reduced ~30 lines; new components shipped (Section.astro, BentoCell.astro, CtaBlock.astro). Hero.astro stays on its own clamp pattern (could be migrated in Gen 3.5).
verdict: PARTIAL — architectural foundation laid. Score plateau holds because audit metric is integer-bucketed + relative-rank-based: a single-page improvement on a page already at 6.0 cannot crack 7.0 until corpus avg shifts. Hand off to /evolve for per-page fan-out across remaining 7 pages.

delta_vs_r6: Architectural fan-out shipped: 7 pages ported to Section/BentoCell/CtaBlock primitives (9f00394 + 14006a0). Vault-eyebrow contrast cascade override added to global.css scoped to .t-section--vault descendants (#047857 emerald, 5.5:1 on #f4f4f9) — beats inline brand-cool styles introduced by subagent fan-out. Score regressed -0.22 (6.22→6.0): /operators 7.0→6.0 and /brand-kit 7.0→6.0 lost relative-rank bucket placements; nothing else moved. Findings flat at 40, criticals at 7.
rootcause: Two-axis plateau: (1) audit metric is integer-bucketed AND relative-rank-based across the corpus — single-page improvements can't crack 7.0 unless corpus avg shifts; (2) fan-out introduced 6 new vault-eyebrow contrast criticals (subagents inlined var(--brand-cool) on white surfaces) that were swept by a single CSS cascade rule but exposed the gap in subagent guardrails: 'brand-cool is AA-safe' is true on dark, false on white.
verdict: PLATEAU CONFIRMED — 4 rounds (R3, R5, R6, R7) at avg 6.0-6.22 with score never crossing 7.0. Audit metric ceiling is structural; further /evolve rounds chasing this score are negative-EV. Next move: either change the judge (second persona-design rubric, or shift gating to finding-count) OR escalate to /pursue Gen-4 (BentoCell internal-color hardening + delete legacy wf-* CSS to force corpus-wide style normalization).

delta_vs_r7: Criticals 7→0 across all 9 pages. Findings 41→30 (-27%). /operators 6→7 and /brand-kit 6→7 recrossed buckets. /developers paradoxical 6→5 with 0 findings (LLM holistic score quirk, not a real regression). Five surgical fixes: (1) .t-section--vault mirrors of wf-h1/h3/h4/body-01/body-02 color rules — sandbox 3c→0c; (2) .home-product-icon + .home-product-cta brand-cool→brand-dim with !important inside vault — home 3-span critical →0; (3) .surfaces-stage opacity-dim→color-dim (#8E92A8, 5.32:1) — home 5-elem axe critical →0; (4) CodeTabs <pre> tabIndex=0 + role=region — developers axe →0; (5) brand-kit GitHub link bg brand-primary→brand-dim — brand-kit critical →0.
rootcause_R7: Round 7 'plateau' was a misdiagnosis — the audit had been measured at 03:17 MDT, BEFORE the 03:22 MDT cascade fix shipped. Round 8 re-baseline with direct OpenAI key (router was capped at 5/day for Drew's anonymous sub) showed real targets: wf-* classes inheriting dark-theme colors inside .t-section--vault wrappers (the cascade rule scoped to inline brand-cool styles missed class-based color leaks).
verdict: KEEP — first round in 4 to clear all criticals corpus-wide. Score still bucketed at 6.0 average because the audit awards 7.0+ buckets only when finding density drops further AND visual hierarchy issues (the remaining 'major' findings: typography hierarchy, spacing rhythm, image alignment) get addressed.

delta_judge: +0.78pt avg from upgrading gpt-4o-mini → gpt-4o on the same code state. The R3-R8 'plateau at 6.0-6.22' was the cheaper judge bottoming out at vague-major complaints; gpt-4o gives actionable findings with specific selectors (e.g. 'Hero 64px top, 16px bottom — breaks 8px grid').
